text:
The Mafia Community (マフィアン・コミュニティー, Mafian Komyunitī), also referred to as just the Mafia (マフィア, Mafia) Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 72 or Syndicate/Community (コミュニティー, Komyunitī), Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 73 is a collection of crime syndicates, groups, and individuals spread all over the world.

Overview

Hierarchy and Territories

The Mafia seems to have a traditionalist structure in general, with a council of leaders (likely coming from powerful Mafia families) at the top and several families one level below (with the presence of families subordinate to others).

The council of leaders are the Ten Dons, and each have their own turf in ten regions on six continents. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 74 

Ten Dons
Shadow Beasts
Bean
Ritz Family
Nostrade Family
Zenji
Kakin Mafia
Xi-Yu Family
Heil-Ly Family
Cha-R Family
Buor Family
Rottfelli
Trink

After the events involving the Phantom Troupe and the Mafia in Yorknew City, the Mafia power structure was remapped, allowing Kakin's Mafia to get exclusive rights to the New Continent. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 377 

Business

While the Mafia's business and interests can be legal, much of it is composed of crimes and illegal operations, including:
commodity control in certain places;
human trafficking;
distribution of black market goods;
and smuggling. 

However, a family’s operations can be entirely legal, such as those of the Nostrade Family which include:
personal security details;
and gambling. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 343 

Murder seems to be common in the Mafia, but as a way of doing business and not as a business in itself, which is why the Ten Dons contracted professional assassins to deal with the Phantom Troupe. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 95 

The Mafia controls the Underground Auction, during which time all the Ten Dons gather together to discuss their plans and issue orders, which are carried out by the Shadow Beasts. However, after the multiple incidents involving the Phantom Troupe, the event is now held online. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 

Relations

Yorknew City Government: The Mafia Community has a close-knit relationship with the mayor of Yorknew City, Jitonoda, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 129 paying for 60% of the city’s mayoral election campaign. So when events like the Underground Auction are held, the police work together with the Mafia since the chief of police is the mayor's lapdog. This makes imposing traffic restrictions simple and works to the Mafia’s benefit. 

Meteor City: In exchange for many of their indigenous people with no traceable records as personnel, the Mafia Community "throws away" gold and weapons in the city. This partnership, however, was potentially destroyed by the Phantom Troupe’s multiple massacres. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 105 

Kakin Empire: The three most powerful Mafia families of Kakin have direct links to the Royal Family, and two of them are protective of the royalty and its interests. Also, no other Mafia group but those originating from Kakin appear to be aboard the Black Whale.

Mafia and Nen

The higher-ups in the Mafia Community are no strangers to Nen. The Shadow Beasts, whose members act as bodyguards for the Ten Dons and carry out their orders, each have Nen abilities. Light Nostrade is also aware of the existence of Nen, mentioning that Dalzollene was a Nen user after being surprised by the news of his death, claiming that he could be shot by ten bullets and walk away unscathed. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 79 

Most Mafia members, however, seem to be unaware of Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 75 

Although it has only been mentioned within Kakin's Mafia, the concept of "balance" is probably something appreciated and present in the whole Community. As Nen is considered a dangerous threat, there are unspoken rules such as hiding and not proliferating it in order to maintain the status quo. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 378 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 379 

Onior Longbao, the boss of the Xi-Yu Family, is unaware of Nen when asked by Zhang Lei, but says that that some of their younger members use strange powers. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 390 

Members

Plot

Yorknew City arc

At the Nostrade's mansion, Dalzollene informs the bodyguard applicants that they have one month to acquire an item from the list given to them. However, before they can leave, they are ambushed by eleven assailants. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 67 As it turns out, all of the assailants are later revealed to be made of Nen created by Tocino, a current Nostrade Family employee who was ordered to test the applicants. After discovering that Squala is also a Nostrade employee, Kurapika, Basho, Melody, and Baise successfully escape the mansion. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 68 The applicants each return with one of the requested items and are hired as bodyguards for Neon, with Dalzollene telling them that their first mission is to escort her to her hotel room in Yorknew City. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 70 

As the bodyguards escort her by airship and then by car, Neon uses her ability to write out fortunes for several Mafia members, including Rottfelli and Trink. At the Phantom Troupe's hideout on the outskirts of the city, Chrollo tells the members that they will steal all the items at the Underground Auction. Uvogin becomes ecstatic at the possibility of antagonizing Mafia members from all across the world, and Chrollo gives them permission to kill anyone who gets in their way. 

At the Hotel Beitacle, after telling Light about some concerning fortunes, Dalzollene briefs the bodyguards on their mission at the auction and lists the items that they need to obtain for Neon. After Dalzollene divides them into groups and they go their separate ways, Neon throws a temper tantrum after being told that she won’t be allowed to attend the auction. After Light tells him that he is making his way to Yorknew, Dalzollene calls the bodyguards and asks for updates on the security around the Cemetery Building. Kurapika and Melody then have a lengthy conversation about each of their pasts. Inside the building, after Baise wonders why so many of the Mafia members are attending the auction in person, Tocino explains that they can earn points for their family and that the community receives 5% of the proceeds as a commission. He adds that it's a way for them to show how wealthy they are, explaining that some families even end up bankrupt after getting into a bidding war. 

Several Troupe members head to the auction while the rest stay behind at the hideout. As the auction starts, Franklin and Feitan suddenly walk out onto the stage of the crowded auditorium. Feitan announces that they will all be killed as Franklin uses his Double Machine Gun to massacre nearly all the guests, with Shizuku killing any who escape and using Blinky to get rid of all the evidence. One Mafia member remains barely alive and declares that the Mafia will track down them and their families, promising that they will all be tortured. However, Franklin quickly kills him as Feitan wonders what family is. 

Mafia members rush inside the Cemetery Building and discover both the auditorium and vault empty, sending out a message to all the families in Yorknew to follow any suspicious aircrafts. They also announce that a reward will be given to the family that catches the thieves, stressing that they should keep the leader alive. After Kurapika and Melody update him on the situation, Dalzollene decides to gather the other bodyguards and chase after the thieves along with dozens of other Mafia members. Kurapika worries that the ambition of the Mafia families will prevent any cooperation between them. As the Troupe members make their escape in a hot-air balloon, Uvogin contacts Chrollo and updates him on the situation, speculating that there might be a Judas among them. However, Chrollo convinces him that there isn't and adds that the Mafia's security would have been tighter if they knew about their plan. He guesses that they only received a vague tip and that the higher-ups trusted the source enough to act on it. Uvogin then tells Chrollo about the Ten Dons and the Shadow Beasts, adding that before they killed the auctioneer, he told them that a member named Owl showed up and somehow left empty-handed after emptying the entire vault. Chrollo guesses that he has a Nen ability similar to Shizuku's and that the Mafia knows that Nen is involved after 500 guests suddenly vanished. Uvogin asks for permission to fight them, and Chrollo tells him to cause enough of a disturbance to draw out the Shadow Beasts. The Mafia members on the ground then spot the Troupe's hot-air balloon and give chase. 

The Mafia members see that the balloon is heading toward the Gordeau Desert, and after seeing that they aren't using an airship, Bean wonders where the guests are and guesses that it's more than just a heist. He then tells the others to call the Ten Dons so that they can send the Shadow Beasts. After their balloon is shot down in the desert and dozens of Mafia members order them to come down from the high ledge, Uvogin assures his comrades that he will take care of them, sliding down the cliffside and confronting them. A Mafia member points a gun in Uvogin's face and asks if they kidnapped the guests, which Uvogin confirms. Uvogin simply smiles when the man asks about their leader, and when he fires at point blank range, Uvogin catches the bullet with his teeth and breaks the man's neck with a single swipe. He then begins to massacre the other Mafia members, surviving both a shot from a sniper rifle and an anti-tank missile. Kurapika and the other Nostrade bodyguards watch the carnage from afar when four Shadow Beasts arrive to deal with the Troupe. Worm, Leech, Rabid Dog, and Porcupine all confront Uvogin. 

Worm is the first to be incapacitated by Uvogin's Big Bang Impact. After Uvogin is distracted by Porcupine and immobilized by Rabid Dog, Leech begins to insert his leeches into the wound on Uvogin's shoulder. However, Uvogin kills him by biting off a portion of his head and then spits out a fragment of his skull like a bullet to kill Rabid Dog. With Porcupine still attached to his arm and unable to use physical attacks against him, Uvogin lets out a loud scream right toward him that results in his death. As Shalnark explains how to remove the leeches from his system, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 76 Kurapika takes advantage of Uvogin's paralysis and captures him with Chain Jail, quickly driving away from the canyon with the other bodyguards. Machi immediately uses a needle and a Nen thread to track Uvogin. She and four other members then chase after Kurapika by car, but just before they can catch up with him, the remaining Shadow Beasts arrive and stop them. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 77 

The bodyguards then take Uvogin to a building somewhere in the city and prepare to torture him to learn what happened to the auction items and attendees. Uvogin assures them that the items were already gone when they arrived and agrees to spare their lives if they release him, but when he reveals that the auction attendees were all killed, Kurapika punches him in the face and forces his comrades to calm him down. After Dalzollene informs someone that they captured one of the thieves, he learns that all the Shadow Beasts were killed. With Kurapika out to meet with Hisoka and the other guards sleeping, Dalzollene welcomes the Mafia members who arrive to take Uvogin. However, they are revealed to be the other Troupe members, with Phinks quickly killing Dalzollene. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 78 

Back at the Hotel Beitacle, the bodyguards try to call Dalzollene and assume that he’s dead after he fails to answer. The others nominate Kurapika to be the new head bodyguard, and Kurapika contacts Light, who puts him in charge and orders him to protect Neon until he arrives. Shalnark discovers that the building where Uvogin was kept is owned by a dummy company for the Nostrade Family. He then finds two other properties and learns that their employees are staying at three hotels, assuring Uvogin that he should be able to find the chain user by tomorrow. Kurapika tells Squala and Melody that the Hunter Website lists all the properties owned by the Nostrade Family as well as all its employees. He then tells them to move Neon to another room and to stay there while he waits for Uvogin. 

Uvogin eventually tracks Kurapika to the hotel and allows him to choose where he wants to die. Elsewhere in the city, Gon, Killua, and Leorio attend a conditional auction and learn that bounties have been put on the heads of the Troupe members by the Mafia, seizing the opportunity to look for them. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 80 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 81 After Kurapika fights and kills Uvogin, he calls Melody who tells him that Neon's father has been delayed. After killing the remaining Shadow Beasts, the Troupe captures Owl and takes him back to their hideout, torturing him in order to acquire the auction items. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 84 

After Light arrives and the bodyguards tell him about the situation, he praises their quick thinking and says that he will be sending Neon home now that the auction has been canceled, telling Melody and Basho to accompany her back to the mansion. However, once Neon leaves the room, Light reveals that the auction will reopen and that the Ten Dons have hired assassins to eliminate the Troupe. He then asks Kurapika to join the assassin team so that they won’t be able to take all the credit. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 89 

Once Killua is cornered by Phinks and Nobunaga, he says that he was following them because the Mafia put a large bounty on their heads and that websites were offering rewards just for their location. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 91 At the Troupe’s hideout, Shalnark decides that it’s time for them to go on the offensive. He hands out a list of the Nostrade Family’s employees and tells them to memorize their faces, especially those of Neon’s bodyguards, since they were the ones who captured Uvogin. He suggests that they work in pairs to find them and that they should meet back at the hideout later that night. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 92 Accompanied by Melody, Basho, and her attendants, Neon is seen on a shopping spree at Lingon Airport. After pretending to use the bathroom, she puts on a disguise and leaves on her own, intent on going to the auction by herself after her father lied to her. An unknown figure holding a photograph of Neon is then seen following her. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 94 

At the meeting of assassins, Zenji tells them that their job is to kill the Phantom Troupe if they attack the auction again, leaving the method up to them. After floor plans and maps are handed out and the meeting ends, Zenji confronts Light and reminds him of how quickly he has risen up the ranks, mocking him as well. The two then get into a fight which Kurapika breaks up. Light tells Kurapika about the importance of information, especially when it comes to future events, and is confident that he will be able to continue increasing his prestige with Neon’s ability. Melody calls Kurapika and tells him about Neon’s disappearance, with Kurapika guessing that she is making her way to the auction. Light calls someone and tells them to have the police look out for a young girl trying to pass the checkpoints. As Neon is seen with Chrollo entering the perimeter, Light desperately tells someone to put out a notice for his daughter and to print out her picture so they can find her. However, Kurapika uses his Dowsing Chain on a map of the city and easily determines that she is already inside the Cemetery Building. 

After Neon uses her ability to tell Chrollo’s fortune, the Troupe leader knocks her unconscious as he desperately tells the Mafia members to call for an ambulance. Hesitant at first, Bean remembers that even the Ten Dons are fans of Neon and eventually gives the order. While Light plans to meet Neon on the fifth floor, Kurapika checks the Hunter Website and sees pictures of Neon, Basho, and Baise. He then informs Light and reveals that the auction will be canceled again after the place becomes a battlefield. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 96 

As Chrollo kills the assassins inside the Cemetery Building, all the Troupe members (excluding Nobunaga) kill the police and Mafia members outside in their requiem to Uvogin. In disbelief that there are fewer than ten attackers, Bean tells his men to keep them away from the building. After the ambulance arrives, Bean and the others draw their weapons and hold them at gunpoint. Bean then tells them to follow him and not to try anything. Kurapika sees the Mafia members beginning to panic and realizes that the auction organizers will soon lose control of the situation. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 97 Bean wonders where the reinforcements are, but as he hears screaming and explosions nearby, he realizes that the 2,000 armed men are all being killed. He calls someone and tells them to contact the Ten Dons immediately. As the Mafia members begin to panic and ask for their weapons back, Zeno Zoldyck takes control of the situation and tells them to wait until they take care of the attacker inside the building. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 98 

As Chrollo and the two Zoldycks continue to fight, the worried Mafia members sense the enormous explosion below them. Bean tries to calm them down by informing them that they have made contact with the Dons. He tells them to make their way to the auction hall and that the Dons will speak through a live video feed. With the auditorium now full, one of the Dons appears on the screen and reveals that they have killed the Troupe's leader. As the Mafia members celebrate, the Don tells them to enjoy the auction while the other Troupe members are hunted down, adding that all the Dons will watch the bidding. At the Dons' location, it's revealed that Illumi has killed them all with the help of Maha and Kalluto. Illumi contacts Silva and asks him to tell his client Chrollo that the Dons have been killed. Chrollo wonders why Zeno isn't going to finish him off, and he replies that Dons were their clients and since they're now dead, he is no longer their target. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 100 

After an ambulance transports Neon to a hospital, Chrollo contacts the Troupe members and says that they will ignore the ambulance and go ahead as planned. The announcer begins the auction, and Bean walks backstage, relieved that none of the capos were injured. However, he is quickly killed by Machi after being distracted by Hisoka. It's then revealed that the Troupe has secretly taken over the auction, with Shalnark using his ability to manipulate the announcer and Kortopi creating copies of the items. Kurapika is told by two Mafia members that the Troupe's leader was killed, and Zenji mocks him for wasting his time while the Zoldycks took care of the job. Kurapika proceeds to punch him in the face, declaring that he needs to see it with his own eyes. 

Kurapika and several Mafia members stand beside Chrollo's corpse, with one of them asking where Bean is. Two others are surprised that the Troupe's leader is so young. When one of the men grabs Chrollo by his hair, someone else tells him not to damage his face any more than it is since they plan to post a picture of him on the Internet. After they also find the bodies of Shalnark, Shizuku, Feitan, Machi, and Franklin, one of the Mafia members tells his comrades to find out their identities by checking every database, adding that their bodies should be dissected below the neck. He also tells them to find and kill all their friends and relatives so that it serves as a warning. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 101 

Kurapika quickly returns to the auction hall and bids on the pair of Scarlet Eyes, calling Light to make sure that he can bid whatever is necessary to win them. After learning that Kurapika had to bid 2.9 billion to beat out Zenji, Light is initially concerned but assures himself that the money will return to the Mafia and only increase his prestige. Sitting by Neon in her hospital bed, he also reminds himself that he will easily make the money back with his daughter’s ability, stressing that he just needs to keep her happy. Kurapika picks up the eyes for the Nostrade Family and makes his way to the hospital after a tense exchange with Zenji. After Kurapika delivers the eyes to an ecstatic Neon, Light thanks the bodyguards and says that they can now return to the hotel. He also tells them that there is still one item left to win at the auction and that while he is leaving the day after tomorrow, Neon can stay behind to go shopping. Melody tells Kurapika that they will take care of things so that he can get some rest. Meanwhile, the entire Troupe celebrates their successful plan against the Mafia back at their hideout. 

After six hours of searching, a Mafia hacker is unable to find any information on the deceased Troupe members. He tells two other Mafia members that the dead Spiders don't exist, and after they argue back and forth, the hacker surmises that they must have been born in Meteor City where their births didn't have to be officially acknowledged. After explaining more about the city and how vengeful its citizens can be, he tells the two men that they don't want to fight the Troupe if its members are from Meteor City, adding that the Dons would agree with his assessment. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 102 

After Kurapika learns that the corpses of the Troupe members are fake, Melody contacts him and says that the Ten Dons canceled their bounties on the Troupe after learning that they are from Meteor City. Leorio tries to explain the reasoning to Gon and Killua, pointing out that gold and weapons are provided by a certain organization. Kurapika says that Leorio is referring to the Mafia, leaving Gon and Killua confused. Leorio explains that it's the perfect place to recruit since the people there have no official records. He adds that the two had the ideal partnership until the Troupe ruined it. 

Kurapika tells Leorio that he asked about the auction, but that the Mafia hasn't made any decisions yet. Leorio says that they might reconsider if he tells them that the Spiders are still alive, but Kurapika is confident that the Mafia doesn't want to have any disagreements with Meteor City, guessing that the organization's war against the Troupe is finished. He wonders if the auction will still be held, and Leorio realizes that Kurapika plans to fight the Troupe, wondering how he will do it without the Mafia's help. As his aura swells around him, Kurapika says that he wasn’t counting on it. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 107 

While Melody is accompanying Neon during her shopping spree, Kurapika calls to ask for her help. At the Troupe’s hideout, Shalnark holds up the list of Nostrade employees from the Hunter Website and tells Chrollo that Uvogin recognized the top three. Chrollo tells him that he checked the site yesterday and found not only a picture of Neon, but the list of bodyguards with two new employees. As Chrollo tells Shalnark to check the list again, Franklin wonders why one girl would have seven bodyguards. Chrollo points out that her father only achieved his position by exploiting her fortune-telling ability. Shizuku wonders why he is in Yorknew, and Pakunoda guesses that she came for the auction, causing Chrollo to remember that Neon is a collector of body parts. Shalnark makes the connection to the Scarlet Eyes, and Chrollo says that the chain user was hired by the Nostrade Family because he knew the eyes would be at the auction and Neon would want them. After having Kortopi track the copied eyes, Chrollo uses a map and deduces that they are at the Hotel Beitacle. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 109 

As several Troupe members make their way to the hotel, Killua and Melody track them. Kurapika eventually realizes where they are heading and calls Squala to warn him as he takes only the eyes with him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 110 When Gon and Killua are caught by the Troupe again, the latter feigns shock when Machi mentions that the Mafia’s reward was canceled. After Melody tells Kurapika that Squala isn’t answering his phone, he is shown stuck in traffic and surrounded by Pakunoda, Kortopi, and Nobunaga, who recognizes him from the list and tells him to exit the car. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 111 Pakunoda begins her interrogation, but when Squala asks if she knows that he works for the Nostrade Family, she breaks his arm and orders him to answer her questions. Squala becomes provoked by Pakunoda’s mention of Eliza and turns to face her, causing Nobunaga to kill him for disobeying his order. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 112 Melody then helps Kurapika with the hostage exchange to secure the return of Gon and Killua. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 117 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 118 

Greed Island arc

Melody tells Kurapika that the Underground Auction was canceled and all future ones are also canceled, with the Mafia holding it online from now on. She then informs him of Squala’s death and says that Eliza was overcome with grief since the two were in love. She adds that while the Scarlet Eyes were also taken, Neon was more concerned about Eliza and offered to return home. 

At the Nostrade’s mansion, Neon sits on her bed and wonders why her ability won’t appear anymore. Light suffers a mental breakdown after the loss of his daughter’s ability and wonders how they will make money (especially after paying 2.9 billion for the Scarlet Eyes). Light begs him for advice and angrily blames him, but Kurapika assures him that he will take care of everything. However, Light continues to berate him and wonders why it happened. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 149 

Succession Contest arc

After Steiner is told that the wrung-out bodies were discovered on this side, he asks if there is any record of the victims’ passage to the new world. The IPA Director tells him that some have suggested it’s related to the Mafia because of their circle of acquaintances, but he stresses that testimony from multiple eyewitnesses refutes the theory. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 341 

At an undisclosed location owned by the Nostrade Family, Mizaistom introduces himself and asks to see Kurapika. Linssen informs him that Kurapika is away at the moment and asks him to leave. However, Mizaistom insists on staying to wait for him, and three lackeys prepare to make him leave, but are restrained by Mizaistom's Cross Game. Linssen begrudgingly tells Mizaistom that Kurapika is downstairs, warning Mizaistom that Kurapika is in a bad mood. Mizaistom asks Linssen whether working for the mafia is his real job since he's also a Hunter, but Linssen explains that the family isn't a crime syndicate since they get all their income legally through personal security details and gambling, and that they pay their taxes. 

When Kurapika arrives, Mizaistom introduces himself and offers information on the remaining Scarlet Eyes in exchange for Kurapika joining the Zodiacs and accompaning them to the Dark Continent. Not having any leads on their owners thus far, Kurapika agrees, and Mizaistom reveals the Fourth Prince of the Kakin Empire, Tserriednich Hui Guo Rou, to be the owner of all the remaining pairs of eyes, information he randomly came across while doing background checks on the passengers of the Kakin Empire's voyage to the Dark Continent. 

Days after Kurapika joins the Zodiacs, Linssen informs him that six Kakin Princes are hiring bodyguards for the voyage, and that the only information shown are the salaries offered, some of which are continually changing. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 350 Unable to fully identify which Princes are behind the listings, Kurapika hires five acquaintances. Together, they all apply to one listing each to maximize Kurapika's chances of coming into contact with Prince Tserriednich. Kurapika is hired to protect Fourteenth Prince Woble. 

On the second day of the Black Whale’s voyage, two men walk through a passageway towards the central dining hall on Tier 5 and find three members of the Buor Family charging them 5,000 to pass through, having paid off the soldiers in charge to let them do so. The Buor members offer them alternative ways to pay or get food but are forced to pause when Phinks, Franklin, Feitan, and Nobunaga suddenly arrive and tell the Buor members to move. The Buor members try to intimidate them, but find themselves quickly subdued by the Spiders. The Spiders bring them to the dining hall, where the Buor members explain that their family is insignificant compared to Kakin's three big mafia families. The Spiders have the Buor members explain Kakin's three big mafia families to them, and learn that Kakin’s three big families are aboard the ship to secure territory on the New Continent. He adds that each family has a direct line to a Prince, and that the family’s bosses are VIPs on Tier 1, with the henchmen of each family trying to reduce the enemy numbers until the ship lands. Phinks asks him which family he belongs to, and the man replies that they are just freelance thugs and that the big families take a 90% cut from them. He then names the families and their benefactors: the Third Prince for the Xi-Yu Family, the Fourth Prince for the Heil-Ly Family, and the Seventh Prince for the Cha-R Family. He adds that once you pledge yourself to one family, the other two become your mortal enemies. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 

Nobunaga then asks him how to get above Tier 2, but the man says that movement between tiers is impossible. Phinks guesses that there's a middleman between the boss and the henchmen, and Franklin suggests antagonizing all three families, asking which is the strongest. The Buor member replies that the Xi-Yu is strongest in terms of finances and numbers, but then pleads with the Troupe members not to cause any trouble. Franklin guesses that someone must come down to take the cut, telling him to call the man over. The Buor member replies that the next time is Saturday, and Nobunaga says that it will work for the loot, leaving him confused. Phinks tells him not to ask questions and says that their new mission is to find everyone taller than 6'2" and get their room numbers. 

As the Phantom Troupe holds a meeting in the central dining hall, a group from the Cha-R Family surrounds them. Their leader asks them to leave, but Chrollo refuses. The leader stops his subordinates from initiating a fight, and thanks Chrollo for the Troupe's rampage in Yorknew City, which allowed Kakin's mafia families exclusive access to the New Continent. He offers to help Chrollo if they leave. Chrollo accepts, and gives him a loose description of Hisoka. The leader offers to help them check the Royal Army's passenger list, but adds that Hisoka might be an unofficial passenger. He lets the Troupe search Tier 5, indirectly identifying themselves as Cha-R members, and invites them to join their family in exchange for tickets to get to Tiers 3 and 4. Chrollo politely declines. and asks about gaining access to Tier 1, which prompts the Mafia leader to change his tone and insist they leave. As the Spiders walk away, the group's leader asks Sun-bin if the Troupe is dangerous, and he replies that they will reach Tier 1 if they want to get there and that they didn't even try to hide their Nen. The group leader tells Sun-bin to call their young leader and to warn him that there might be a fight. 

The Troupe split to search for Hisoka, and Shizuku asks to join Chrollo. Shizuku asks Chrollo if he can read their fortunes again so that they can hopefully get some clues to find Hisoka. However, Chrollo reveals that the ability disappeared from his book. Shizuku seems to realize what the implication is, and Chrollo gives a general confirmation (i.e., that Neon has died). 

In Tier 1, Onior Longbao (the Xi-Yu boss) and Brocco Li (the Cha-R boss) discuss the Spiders, with the latter mentioning that they couldn't even fit into Meteor City and that it will be difficult to control them since they don't care about life or death. He also mentions that some of his family's younger members are secret fans of theirs. The two bosses summon their underbosses and order them to find Hisoka before the Spiders do. Ken'i, the Cha-R underboss, tells his subordinate to gather the group leaders and offer ten million to anyone who provides useful information, ten million to anyone who finds Hisoka, and another ten million to the leader of the area. Hinrigh, the Xi-Yu underboss, tells his subordinates to obtain the manifest and look through the single male passengers and families of more than three. He guesses that Hisoka has stowed away and that it will be difficult to find him. He then tells his men to ask the Queen to have the soldiers check IDs and tickets. Each underboss tells his men to find Hisoka and assures them that he will take care of the rest. 

Somewhere in Tier 3, the boss of the Heil-Ly Family and illegitimate daughter of Nasubi, Morena Prudo, thanks the members who chose to follow her and tells them that they will destroy everything. Speaking to her 22 members, she uses her Contagion ability on each of them, allowing them to increase their aura and power with every person they kill. She explains that civilians are worth one level, those with abilities ten, and Princes fifty. After she wishes them good luck, she thinks about her past and looks forward to tearing down what she considers a cruel world. 

In Tier 5, Nobunaga, Phinks, and Feitan decide to leave Tier 5 to the Buor Family and move to Tier 4 to search for Hisoka after they retrieve their weapons from the warehouse. After arriving at the warehouse, the three encounter some henchmen of the Cha-R Family, one of whom leads them inside. As the man turns the corner, Nobunaga stops Phinks and Feitan, stating that he suddenly vanished from his En, with Phinks noting that he can no longer feel his presence. They walk down the corridor, which turns out to be a dead end, and then hear a thud from behind them. They find the man lying dead on the floor with his throat cut open. Phinks and Feitan nonchalantly keep searching for the items on their own, ridiculing Nobunaga's concern and telling him that his priorities are weird. As the three continue to talk, the hitman and Heil-Ly member Luini watch them from his portal in the ceiling and describe them as his idols, hoping that they can destroy the world together. In the general passenger area on Tier 3, people crowd around a crime scene as Mizaistom asks how many victims there are. The person tells him that the culprit dressed as a soldier and quickly killed twenty people with a blade, with no sign that Nen was used. After learning that they have one witness, Mizaistom asks to talk to them. 

After the female witness recounts the killer mentioning levels and the number of people, she tells Mizaistom that he somehow escaped from the inside of a locked bathroom. Saying to himself that a Nen ability must be involved, Mizaistom tells the woman to rest in her cabin. However, it’s then revealed that the woman (named Cashew) is a member of the Heil-Ly Family and worked with Luini on the plan so that he could determine how skilled they are at investigating. Cashew tells herself that she will slowly level up and get to know the investigators better. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 379 

Elsewhere, Luini explains his teleportation ability in detail and says he will enjoy his collaboration with the Spiders before he tests whether he can use it to go outside the ship. After retrieving their weapons, Phinks, Nobunaga, and Feitan return to the warehouse entrance and find that the other Mafia members are gone, but Phinks points out that one got away, noting a trail of dry blood and worrying that it's a trap. He then remarks that if someone had wanted to frame them as enemies of the Cha-R Family, the first corpse would have been enough. When Nobunaga replies that the murders may be unrelated to them, Phinks asks him if he believes multiple parties are involved. From Nobunaga's answer, he surmises that the culprit ran into them by chance and decided to use them, but switched back to his original plan when a fight didn't break out. They begin to follow the trail and surmise that Hisoka isn't involved, guessing that the culprit's target is the Cha-R Family. The trail ends at a locked door, and Phinks notices the two security cameras nearby, which he finds odd for Tier 5. After Nobunaga tries to open the door and finds that it's locked, the Cha-R underboss Ken'i Wang appears behind them with four armed subordinates. He asks if they are the ones who attacked the warehouse guards. Still, after the Troupe members say that they weren't involved, Ken'i receives a call from his subordinates about a man who entered their hideout with a wounded warehouse guard and yelled that the Spiders did it. Ken'i is annoyed that someone tried to manipulate the two groups into fighting and guesses that he was a hit man from one of the other families. Phinks speculates that they did so to fulfill an ability condition to teleport into their hideout at a later time, explaining the process in great detail and even mentioning Nen by name. While Ken'i asks the three Troupe members if they want to work together, he tells himself that they are dangerous and must be eliminated. 

Now sitting by himself in the Tier 5 central dining hall as he enjoys a meal, Franklin is approached by Ittoku, the consigliere of the Cha-R Family, and four other Mafia members. As Ittoku sits opposite him, Franklin reminds him that they already moved tables and asks if he has something else to say. Ittoku uses Franklin’s name and wonders if he’s going to look for Hisoka. Franklin replies that he doesn’t waste effort, explaining that since Hisoka is looking to kill them, he will come find him as long as he sits and waits. Ittoku then reveals that two of his warehouse guards were killed and four were taken. Franklin assures him that the Troupe wasn’t involved, but then says that it depends on whether or not the Mafia members started the fight. Ittoku points out that Franklin’s three comrades were guaranteed to receive their weapons, wondering why they would fight them. Franklin then assures him that it wasn’t the Troupe and that someone else took advantage of the situation. Ittoku asks if it was Hisoka, and while Franklin says that it could be, he stresses that it wasn’t the Troupe. As he stands up to leave, Ittoku finally asks if the Troupe boarded the ship just for Hisoka, and while Franklin replies that they are still thieves, he stresses that they won’t take the next step until they find and kill Hisoka. After putting out his cigarette on his own steak, Ittoku apologizes and leaves with his men. 

Inside the Cha-R's hideout, after Ken'i and the Troupe members watch the surveillance footage, Nobunaga points out that the hit man will come to open the other door. Ken'i asks if they want to see what's inside, and Nobunaga refuses to do so for free, offering to kill the hit man. After Ken'i agrees, he introduces the Spiders to the other Cha-R members present. After telling them that the Cha-R has around 250 members on board, the supervisor Tsudonke recognizes the Spiders and keeps it to himself, wondering if he should ask them for an autograph. On an overview of the ship, Phinks and Nobunaga are still seen in the Cha-R’s hideout while Feitan has returned to the entrance of the warehouse (since the three are now working with the Mafia family). Elsewhere, Botobai and the Royal Army are seen sweeping the tiers for a stowaway with a deadly weapon. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 380 At 8 p.m. on the fifth day of the voyage, it’s announced that the stowaway has been captured and that everyone can leave their cabins. It is still unknown whether Luini was the one who was captured. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 381 

On the seventh day of the voyage, Tajao, the assistant to the Cha-R boss, emerges from the locked room in the Cha-R’s hideout, escorted by Ken'i. Phinks objects to the way Ken'i introduces them, making it sound like the Spiders are the ones who requested cooperation. Ken'i explains to Tajao that eight of their men have gone missing so far, and the same is happening to the Xi-Yu Family. The total number of missing workers is over 300, and Ken'i believes that the hitman who murdered civilians in Tier 3 has come down with accomplices to the lowest tier to keep killing. Tajao agrees that quick action is needed, but wonders how this involves the Troupe. Phinks explains that they were looking for someone named Hisoka, but that he wasn't in Tier 5. He says that if Tajao can get permission from the Xi-Yu to let them search Tier 4, they will kill the hitman. Phinks describes the hitman's ability and that the hitman is interested in the door that Tajao and Ken'i came through, guessing that it's connected to a higher tier. Tajao says that the Heil-Ly have their own door through Tserriednich, but Phinks guesses that maybe the hit man is planning to take out the Cha-R boss. Ken'i adds that Tserriednich may also be wary of Morena since she recently took over as the Heil-Ly boss, asking Tajao to contact their own boss to confirm whether Morena is in Tier 1. Tajao agrees to do so and tells the Spiders that if she isn't up there, they won't be able to get through, explaining that things will turn to all-out war. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 384 

Ken'i clarifies the situation by explaining that if a Mafia member wishes to make a hit or raid on another family, they must let their boss know first. Then the bosses strike a deal afterward to smooth things over. Tajao adds that this must be done within 24 hours of the hit/raid and that the boss had to be away from their home turf when the initial incident occurred. Nobunaga realizes that the only place away from their turf are the first two tiers, which Tajao confirms. Even though it's been two days since the first murder, it's still fine as long as Morena was in Tiers 1 or 2. But Tajao stresses that if Morena was in Tier 3 and she still hadn't made the call, then a war would inevitably break out. He goes on to say that if the Spiders kill the hitman, they will personally be at war with the Heil-Ly, and Nobunaga is fine with that. Ken'i and Tajao emphasize that it means the Troupe won't be able to get information or help from the low-level thugs anymore. Nobunaga comes up with a quick solution and offers to take out the Heil-Ly boss personally, pointing out that it will solve the issue of Morena causing problems and that Tserriednich may even thank them. Phinks asks them to verify Morena's location while they gather the other Spiders and hunt down the hitman first. Ken'i and Tajao give each other a look, both understanding how dangerous the Troupe can be. The Xi-Yu and Cha-R bosses sit in their respective rooms in Tier 1, but Morena's room is shown to be empty. All three are known as "second-track fakers", illegitimate heirs to the throne who are allowed to live and compensated under the table as long as they stay out of the spotlight and obey the monarchy system absolutely. 

In Tserriednich's quarters, the Fourth Prince is on the phone with someone who asks about Morena's location. He says that she isn't in the VVIP area and has probably been in the lower tiers since they boarded, hidden with help from her butler. He tells the person to do as they please and that he will do something about it when he has time. 

On the tenth day of the voyage, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 Zhang Lei contemplates his Guardian Spirit Beast and its abilities and decides that he likely needs to win the succession war without its assistance. He makes his way to Onior's room to consult him about the succession war. Zhang Lei shares that three of his siblings have already died, and asks Onior whether he knows anything about Guardian Spirit Beasts or Nen. Onior does not know, but mentions that some of his younger members have weird powers, and offers to ask around. He tells Zhang Lei that King Nasubi survived his war by being patient, and says he would look into Nasubi's own succession war. Before leaving, Zhang Lei asks to know how long Onior would take, and Onior tells him about the conflict in the lower decks, expecting it to take a while to fully investigate. As Zhang Lei leaves, Onior gets a call from Hinrigh, who shares that the Heil-Ly's actions are more serious than expected. Onior is surprised to hear that the Spiders are helping them, and asks if they might be trying to get access to the hideout. Hinrigh suggests crushing the Heil-Ly before dealing with the Spiders, but Onior insists that Hinrigh continue searching for the Hisoka to control the Spiders. Onior tells Hinrigh to let the Spiders search Tier 4 for Hisoka after verifying that Hisoka is not on Tier 4. He then tells Hinrigh to head to Tier 3, the Heil-Ly's territory, to search for Hisoka, and to find and kill Morena while he's there. 

Hinrigh gathers subordinates Zakuro and Lynch to resume their search for Hisoka in Tier 3, and tells them of their new goal to crush the Heil-Ly. Two Heil-Ly members on the lookout at a food stand spot them, and the woman leaves to tell Morena. Hinrigh notices the two looking at them and sends Zakuro and Lynch to approach the man as he tails the woman. Hinrigh follows the female Heil-Ly member but is forced to stop after she walks into a civilian area with guards who stop Hinrigh and escort him away. Zakuro and Lynch approach the man, but Zakuro's neck is suddenly slashed by a new Heil-Ly man. The original Heil-Ly man manages to kick Lynch to the ground, but is distracted when he sees Zakuro defeat his opponent with his Bloody Mary. Lynch takes the opportunity to use her Body and Soul to defeat her opponent and learn information about the Heil-Ly. The guards follow him to meet Zakuro and Lynch. Hinrigh lets them know that despite being Heil-Ly members, they're listed as civilians, further complicating their situation. The guards see Zakuro and Lynch standing over the "civilians", and let them go if they leave Tier 3. Hinrigh thanks them and gives them a small bribe, touching their weapons as he does so. They walk away, and the two guards are killed by Hinrigh's Biohazard shortly after. After the Xi-Yu members see the guards killed, they resume their search for Hisoka and Morena. 

Hinrigh splits from Zakuro and Lynch. He goes after the Heil-Ly woman and the rest of the Heil-Ly while Zakuro and Lynch continue the search for Hisoka. Zakuro and Lynch find a quiet hallway without people and activate his Bloody Mary for surveillance. Hinrigh returns back to the crowded areas of Tier 3, where he was forced to stop tailing the woman, surrounded by birds, and gets a guard to help him look at the footage recorded by the various passengers in the area. On a camcorder, he finds a video of the woman on taking the only access route to the standard cabins on Tier 3. He gives the owners some money in exchange for the camcorder and uses Biohazard to disguise it as a cat to secretly record the access route. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 391 

Knowing that the Heil-Ly are not behaving as normal, Hinrigh suspects that the Heil-Ly members are throughout the Tier instead of staying at their hideout and continuing on foot to find them. Heil-Ly members Padaille, Quorolle, and Tevelares spot Hinrigh walking around, and come up with a simple plan for Padaille to engage him first and make an opening for Quorolle and Tevelares to kill Hinrigh. They wonder how the ten levels rewarded for killing a Nen user would be split, but Padaille decides to just engage Hinrigh and find out. Padaille transforms his right hand with his Fistful of Weapons and quietly approaches Hinrigh from behind. Right as he swings, Padaille's hand is stopped by a handcuff, and he realizes that Hinrigh has transformed handcuffs into pigeons to help protect himself. Padaille's hands are handcuffed behind his back, and Hinrigh taunts Quorolle and Tevelares into engaging him. Quorolle and Tevelares draw guns and begin shooting, but Hinrigh easily defends with his pigeons and his Ken. Suddenly, Padaille lunges at Hinrigh, having transformed his right hand into a drill bit with his Fistful of Weapons to slip out of the handcuffs. Hinrigh cannot react fast enough to dodge and is forced to block the attack with his left hand, which is pierced with Padaille's transformed hand. Hinrigh kicks Padaille away, and another pigeon-handcuff handcuffs his left ankle to his left hand, restricting his movements. Seeing Quorolle and Tevelares staying at a safe distance away, Padaille transforms his hand one final time using his Fistful of Weapons into an axe to chop through the handcuff chain, but Hinrigh catches his hand as he swings and forces the axe into the back of his head, killing him. He looks around at the gathering crowd and hopes that Misha can get him out of being held up, as Quorolle and Tevelares make a retreat to avoid being caught and interrogated, and to consult Morena. 

The crowd panicked, having assumed that this was the same murderer that had already been claimed to be caught. They tell soldiers approaching to address the situation where the shooters went. A soldier tells the crowd that this quarrel was between mafia families. The crowd sees Padaille rising and is surprised, having thought he was dead. The soldier asks Padaille if he's alright, and if he wants to press charges, and he declines. The soldier confirms with Hinrigh that he's a Xi-Yu leader, and lets Padaille go after confirming that he's a civilian. The soldier leans close to Hinrigh under the pretense of reminding him that he can't attack civilians, but offers to sell him information on Morena's location. Hinrigh agrees to pay 50,000,000, with 30,000,000 in advance if the soldier personally escorts him there. He leaves, and the soldier declares that the situation is resolved for the general public. Padaille continues walking away, being possessed by Xi-Yu member Misha Hao's Zombie Girl, in order to quietly dispose of his corpse. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 392 

Zakuro and Lynch resume their search for Hisoka by using Zakuro's Bloody Mary as surveillance for tall people, and Lynch's Body and Soul to confirm their identities. They come across Hisoka, but before Lynch can confirm his identity, Hisoka knocks her out. Hisoka lets Zakuro know he's on the way to watch a movie before knocking him out. After they regain consciousness, Zakuro lets Hinrigh know of their findings and then rejoins Lynch on her search for Chrollo. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 393 Tsudonke learns about a fight between the Xi-Yu and 'civilians' on Tier 3 by asking for information at the stores there. Ken'i and the Cha-R leader who confronted Chrollo discuss and agree that Hinrigh is likely to be involved and decide to head to Area E, the location of the conflict. Ken'i sends Tsudonke and three others to Area C to gather information before heading back to the Cha-R base and updating the Spiders. Ken'i reviews the Spiders' response to the Heil-Ly assassin and Ittoku's advice to get rid of the spiders one by one and concludes that the search for Hisoka should be prioritized. He tells his family members to prioritize finding Hisoka first, then the Heil-Ly next, and warns them of the various dangers before they split to search. 

Back at the Cha-R base on Tier 5, a Cha-R underling watches the cameras to welcome the search team when they return. The Spiders consider finding Franklin, which would allow them to split into pairs and search outside without any of them being alone. They hear a knock on the door, and one of their underlings goes to open their door, but is stopped by the one watching the security cameras, who calls the Spiders to look. The Spiders enter the security room and see a hand reaching through a wall on the cameras, knocking on the door. The Spiders interpret him as trying to toy with them. On another monitor, they see the Cha-R search party returning. Phinks opens the door from the security room to find the other Cha-R members missing in their hideout. Nobunaga tells the camera watchman to remain in the security room, as an opening appears in the door. The Heil-Ly assassin sticks his head and upper body through the opening he made, further teasing the Spiders as he does so. Realizing he's starting to annoy the Spiders, he backs off, asking them to work together with the Heil-Ly to defeat the other mafia, the Kakin royals, and go on a rampage back in the world. Before he can react, Nobunaga stabs him through the head, killing him. His portal ability stops working and cleaves his body in half, and his upper body falls to the floor in the Cha-R base. The search team returns and Nobunaga opens the door for them. He explains that their missing underlings were killed by the Heil-Ly assassin, and point to his corpse. The Spiders declare their new goal of personally crushing the Heil-Ly, and resume their search for Hisoka, telling Tsudonke that they would come back later to learn about what's behind the door. 

In a closed room filled with corpses, Heil-Ly members Daemon, Gelato, Perigord, Bille, Voconte, and Matvere look at Luini's severed lower half and try to discuss what to do with the loss of Luini, their most efficient way of hunting for prey to level up. Tevelares and Quorolle knock and open the door, and ask if anyone has become an emitter, strong enough to destroy nen-reinforced pigeon-handcuffs. Tevelares and Quorolle follow Gelato to meet Morena, where Gelato asks for advice about Nen abilities. 

Civilians get up and leave a theatre as they see Hinrigh come and sit next to Hisoka. Hinrigh explains that the Xi-Yu, the Cha-R, and the Spiders have joined forces against the Heil-Ly, and asks Hisoka to remain in Tier 1 until the conflict between the three families finishes, offering him a room in the VVIP area. Hinrigh tells Hisoka that he would be forced to join the Spiders in a fight against him, and makes clear that the Xi-Yu have no intention of getting in between him and the Spiders. Hisoka agrees after letting Hinrigh know that he would not back down if he was confronted by the Troupe. 

The soldier who offered Hinrigh information on Morena, named Maizan, waits around for him and is approached by Ken'i and the Cha-R. Ken'i sees the bloodstains on the floor and asks the soldier what he's plotting with Hinrigh since it's beneath the pay grade of a corporal to stand guard by himself. Maizan offers the same terms to Ken'i, who agrees, and he sends an underling of his to fetch the money. Connelly, a Xi-Yu lieutenant, returns with the money, and the party heads off to Room 3101 in Tier 3 first-class cabins after Hinrigh returns. Maizan knocks on the door and is greeted by an old man, who backs away and sits on the bed after Maizan instructs him to at gunpoint. He steps into the room and backs off. Maizan steps into the room, and after waiting a moment, Hinrigh and Ken'i look into the room but do not see Maizan. They only see the man who denies doing anything wrong and invites them in. They ask the man where he went, and the man suggests he went to the bathroom, before inviting them in again. Hinrigh and Ken'i are suspicious, and Hinrigh threatens him by throwing a knife next to his head, asking him to come over to them. The man stands up, and starts to walk to the bathroom instead. Hinrigh throws a knife into his leg, and the man cries out, but Hinrigh asks him where his real body is, noticing the absence of blood near the stab wound. The man gives up the act and vanishes into Nen. Hinrigh and Ken'i discuss having the Spiders deal with Heil-Ly when a Xi-Yu underling walks into the room to retrieve Hinrigh's knife and vanishes immediately. Hinrigh concludes that the trap teleports people who enter the room, and incorrectly concludes that it had been set by the same person kidnapping their men, but is corrected by Ken'i, who tells him that it was another person since the Spiders had already killed the original kidnapper. Ken'i leaves to get the Spiders to investigate the base, and Hinrigh takes the opportunity to follow up on his leads in the meantime, returning to watch the recordings on the cat-camcorder. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 394 

Tassi only has a moment to look around and notice that he'd been teleported before he is killed by Bille, who then divines his Nen and finds out he is a Conjurer. Terebellum throws Tassi's corpse to Montblanc and Chiffon Toto to be disposed of. Several Heil-Ly members brief Morena and let her know that their liaison with Tserriednich has gone silent. Morena suspects that the prince is on to them, and Sodom and Matvere leave to retrieve one of Tserriednich's personal soldiers for Morena to "recruit" and infect with Contagion to indirectly track Tserriednich's movements. Morena instructs Orarge and Yokotani to keep watch over the door, and to invite any of Tserriednich's men in. Notre and Soufflé are instructed to go back to Room 3131 and check on Room 3101 from outside. Morena calls Dogman over and gives him the most important role, to find the item she's looking for. Dogman and Morena agree that he should be prioritized for levels, and get past level 50. 

Several soldiers break into the Heil-Ly office and find it abandoned. They find the bodies of the remaining two Cha-R warehouse guards killed by Luini there, and their Corporal Gipper decides to use it to justify their break-in with a faked story about trying to halt a conflict but being interrupted by a Heil-Ly member firing at them, who they followed. Knowing the status of the Heil-Ly members being registered as citizens, the Corporal decides to get the assistance of the Xi-Yu and Cha-R in sweeping through Tier 3 to find Morena, before a clueless career bureaucrat declares it a mafia hit on a civilian, which would force the police to get involved. Private Second Class Otocin asks Gipper if they should make a plan regarding Nen with information obtained from Borksen in the Royal Quarters security detail. Gipper responds by berating Otocin's choice to abbreviate Tserriednich's name. Private Second Class Momolly gently reproaches Otocin for getting Borksen into Gipper's line of fire. Gipper hears Otocin abbreviate Tserriednich's name again in his conversation with Momolly and flares up at him again. As they continue their patrol, the soldiers consider the possibility the Hunter Association will get involved and wipe out the mafia, instigating a bigger conflict between the government and the mafia. Momolly brings up her theory that Tserriednich is learning Nen, and the group considers how best to protect themselves. At the same time, they individually think back on their memories as friends with Tserriednich in military school. The group catches up with Borksen, who confirms Momolly's theory, and the group makes plans in case they get caught by Morena, who they suspect uses Nen as well. 

Hinrigh watches his recordings and sees Bille and Perigord (who had earlier attacked Zakuro and Lynch when they first entered Tier 3) walk through the only hallway toward the standard cabins twice, several hours apart. Suspecting that the teleportation trap in Room 3101 was created with a Nen ability, Hinrigh theorizes that the Heil-Ly are using a Nen ability to transit to their hideout. Hinrigh regroups with Ken'i and Gipper's group, where they share information and decide how to proceed. Ken'i shares that Gipper's group had discovered the body of a warehouse guard, and Gipper's group confirms that the Heil-Ly were not in their office. Ken'i further shares that Nobunaga and the Spiders are heading there to check out the secret hideout behind Room 3101, and finishes by sharing that he plans to go after Bille and Perigord after Hinrigh's discovery. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 395 

The Spiders arrive at Room 3101 and proceed to check out the secret hideout behind it from Room 3102 and see signs of recent habitation. They cut into Room 3101 from a wall behind it safely and after some more experimentation conclude that the trap activates only when one walks from the hallway through the doorway of Room 3101. Feitan is about to step into the trap, when Ken'i and Hinrigh approach and stop him. Ken'i introduces Hinrigh to the Spiders, and Hinrigh proposes a way to identify the location of the Heil-Ly base with a location transmitter and tracker without the Spiders having to step directly into the trap. Hinrigh uses Biohazard to transform the transmitter into an oyster which he swallows, and passes the receiver to Phinks before stepping through the trap. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 398 

Hinrigh finds himself teleported right in front of a door. He immediately turns in case someone sneaks up behind him, but notices he is in a big abandoned room with several doors. He notices fresh bloodstains and decides to stay on alert. He senses something behind him and leaps away, but sees that Nobunaga has teleported behind him to join him. Hinrigh and Nobunaga conclude that they've teleported into the Heil-Ly base, after Nobunaga tries to damage the wall and notices a strong Nen protecting it. 

Hinrigh and Nobunaga open the doors one by one, and find only bathroom facilities and a laundry room with nothing more than recent signs of habitation until they open the door they faced when first teleported. Behind it, they see a big lounge area with nine Heil-Ly members in it - Gelato, Soufflé, Orarge, Perigord, Chiffon, Montblanc, and Terebellum sitting at a table in the middle, and Voconte sitting on a couch placed against the left wall of the room. The Heil-Ly members ignore the door opening and continue their casual opening. Hinrigh calls out to them and asks them questions, but they ignore him after looking at him and continue their conversation. Nobunaga and Hinrigh interpret their actions as confirmation that the doorway has another teleportation trap and decide to take action from the doorway to force the Heil-Ly to act. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 399 

Hinrigh begins by throwing a knife into Terebellum's head, but he's able to use his Damage: "Sweet Home" to displace the blade of the knife into a can he was holding in his left hand. Annoyed, Terebellum stands up and prepares to engage them, but Soufflé grabs hold of his left arm to tell him to sit, reminding him they were told not to engage. Hinrigh throws another knife at Terebellum's torso, but he dodges, throwing Soufflé away in the process. The knife continues its trajectory and flies towards Perigord, who activates his aura to test his ability, but is stopped by Orarge, who blocks the knife with his book. Terebellum chastises Soufflé for touching him, and Perigord thanks Orarge for stopping it, who reminds Perigord to keep his head down since he was chosen as the "Organ". Soufflé leaves, and Terebellum calls out to her, asking her to call the authorities, as Yokotani stands up to introduce himself to Hinrigh and Nobunaga. Yokotani stands a safe distance from the door and starts to propose a way to settle the matter, but Nobunaga throws his katana into Yokotani's forehead, interrupting him. Terebellum manages to touch Yokotani in time and takes the damage for him, letting Nobunaga figure his ability out. Yokotani apologizes to Terebellum for forcing him to expose his ability to the enemy, and activates A Battle of Wits: "LSDF", having fulfilled its activation conditions by introducing himself to Hinrigh and Nobunaga. 

The seven Nen conjured puppets of A Battle of Wits: "LSDF" rush towards Hinrigh and Nobunaga, and get stronger after being attacked by them. Hinrigh and Nobunaga prepare to leave, expecting that the puppets are invincible, but Nobunaga is captured before he can do so, and forced through the doorway, confirming the trap. Hinrigh runs and regurgitates the transmitter, hiding it inside the laundry room. He is forced through the doorway and finds himself teleported back to Room 3101, where he sees Nobunaga. Hinrigh decides to take a back seat from the investigation and instead check the floor plans for a place that their hideout could be in, being unable to use his ability anymore that day and having run out of knives. He tells Nobunaga that he plans to make sketches of the Heil-Ly members to show around, narrow down their range of operations, and mobilize his people to gather intelligence. Hinrigh leaves the transmitter hunt to the Spiders, reminding Nobunaga that they need to be careful as long as Morena's ability and the mentioned "Organ" remained unknown. 

Nobunaga rejoins Phinks and Feitan, who head to the passageway between Tiers 3 and 4 to confirm whether the Heil-Ly base is on Tier 2 or Tier 4. While heading down, they make plans to use the Cha-R secret passage up if the base ends up being on the higher tiers and notice the distance from the transmitter increasing, confirming the location of the base on Tier 2. Nobunaga figures out the reason for him feeling something was amiss, that it's unclear how the Heil-Ly are justifying their feud with Morena in the higher tiers away from their official base of operations. Phinks tells him that there is too much they don't know about the Kakin Mafia family customs they don't yet know about, and Feitan suggests going directly to the Cha-R hideout. Nobunaga suggests finding Franklin, but Phinks reminds him that their main priority is finding Hisoka. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 400 

Trivia

Anime and Manga Differences

 

While in the manga the precise divisions of the Mafia territories are not revealed, they are color coded in the 2011 anime adaptation. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 43 (2011)
